about: Knowledge base of creative-app project conventions. The backend reads this at build time to decide (a) which files are interesting, (b) which app a file belongs to, and (c) how to find a file's project root. To support a new tool, add an entry here — no Rust changes needed. Fields: name, icon, category, extensions (file types this app owns), structure ('nested' = projects contain deep subfolders, 'flat' = one document is the project), self_contained (true => the file's own folder IS the project; walking up is skipped), root_marker_exts (a folder is the project root if it directly contains a file with one of these extensions), root_marker_files (a folder is the project root if it directly contains a file/dir with one of these exact names), notes (research summary of the on-disk layout).

apps.name: Unreal Engine
apps.notes: Project root holds <Name>.uproject (and a .sln for C++ projects). Assets live under Content/, C++ under Source/, settings in Config/. Build output in Binaries/, Intermediate/, Saved/, DerivedDataCache/. Deeply nested.

apps.name: Unity
apps.notes: Project root holds Assets/, ProjectSettings/, Packages/ (and Library/ cache). Scenes (.unity), prefabs and materials live under Assets/. Nested.

apps.name: Godot
apps.notes: Project root holds project.godot. Scenes (.tscn), resources (.tres) and GDScript (.gd) sit at or below the root. Nested.

apps.name: Visual Studio
apps.notes: A .sln at the root references one or more .csproj/.vcxproj projects, each usually in its own subfolder. Nested.

apps.name: VS Code
apps.notes: Generic code workspace. The project root is the nearest folder marked by a VCS/package manifest (.git, package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod) or a .vscode folder. Nested.

apps.name: Python
apps.notes: Scripts may be standalone or part of a package. Project root is marked by pyproject.toml/setup.py/requirements.txt or .git; lone scripts fall back to their own folder.

apps.name: Blender
apps.notes: A .blend is a self-contained project file — Blender does not enforce a project tree (textures may sit alongside via relative // paths). The folder the .blend lives in is the project. Flat.

apps.name: Photoshop
apps.notes: Standalone .psd/.psb documents. No project tree — the containing folder is the project. Flat.

apps.name: Illustrator
apps.notes: Standalone .ai documents. The containing folder is the project. Flat.

apps.name: Adobe XD
apps.notes: Standalone .xd documents. The containing folder is the project. Flat.

apps.name: Premiere Pro
apps.notes: A .prproj project file references external media, usually in sibling folders. The .prproj's own folder is treated as the project. Flat.

apps.name: After Effects
apps.notes: An .aep project file references footage from sibling folders. The .aep's own folder is treated as the project. Flat.
